decisionPanels.title: Tonight's decision
decisionPanels.summary: Push Guardian video toward same-day journalist-led vertical explainers; platform discovery is rewarding speed, originality, and recognisable human voice more than generic brand packaging.
actions: Set an ingest-to-first-9:16 SLA for major story packages this week.
Add a pre-publish originality gate: what is new, why now, whose voice carries it?
Test one paired release pattern: Guardian account plus journalist-led version with shared analytics review.

decisionPanels.title: Ops stack watch (iconik/LucidLink/Backlight)
decisionPanels.summary: LucidLink's 2026 moves are now operationally useful: Connect, Guest Access, Directory Management API, and FQDN proxy support all point at lower review friction and less duplicate-copy pain; Backlight continues to signal review speed and repurposing throughput.
actions: Run a 1-week MMR spike on linked-object access assumptions and measure duplicate-copy reduction.
Map Guest Access to one external review handoff where policy allows.
Keep morning manual verification for exact iconik/LucidLink build deltas while anti-bot blocks persist.

decisionPanels.title: Audience growth signal
decisionPanels.summary: Reuters, Nieman, YouTube, TikTok, and Facebook now all point in the same direction: personality-led, modular, multi-format journalism is gaining, while classic search-led traffic keeps weakening.
actions: Treat story packages as modular assets: hook, quote, explainer beat, reporter POV.
Prioritise fast explainers and context cuts over slower polished recuts for same-day relevance.
Track first-hour engagement quality by presenter identity and format, not views alone.

LucidLink Connect: activate existing stores without migration and stream bytes on demand
LucidLink January release: Guest Access, Directory Management API, FQDN proxy support
Backlight resources stream: Iconik Review, Wildmoka AI workflows, supply-chain automation, 2026 media stats
YouTube 2026 priorities: creators as studios, Shorts at 200B daily views, more integrated formats
Facebook unifies video posting: all new videos become reels, reducing format friction for Pages and profiles
TikTok Discover List 2026 highlights storytelling identity and creator-native audience expectations
Reuters Institute 2026: more video, more YouTube/TikTok/AI-platform focus, less faith in classic SEO
Nieman Lab: publishers are being squeezed by AI and creators and pushing journalists to act more like creators
Press Gazette: global publisher Google traffic dropped by a third in 2025

newItems.title: LucidLink January release reframed as operational lever: Guest Access + Directory API + FQDN proxy
Facebook unified video posting added as a concrete distribution workflow signal
Press Gazette traffic decline datapoint added to strengthen urgency around search erosion
Decision panels shifted from watch mode to workflow-speed + journalist-identity execution mode

changes.summary: The model tightened again: audience growth now depends on faster, more human, more modular journalism while MMR reduces workflow drag behind the scenes.
